text:
Once upon a cold, windswept Scottish evening, there was an old librarian named Margaret who lived alone at the top of the hill. She had an extraordinary habit: whenever she saw a spider, she'd rescue it from its web. One particular evening, a gigantic spider caught her attention.

Margaret approached the spider cautiously, placing her hand just above its web. The spider, however, seemed familiar. It was her great-great-grandmother!

"Oh dear," she said tenderly. "How did you end up here?"

The spider web shook noticeably. "I fell out of the sky one foggy night."

Margaret smiled. "You never used to do that."

The spider chuckled. "Times change."

"You have quite the story," Margaret said thoughtfully. She took out a notebook. "Tell me everything."

The spider complied, recounting her daring flight from a stormy sky before revealing Margaret's secret. They spent some time swapping tales until sunset painted the skies.

Later
